data.title: Ulta Beauty shines with solid Q3 performance
data.description:
Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ:ULTA) turned around an ugly second quarter with a strong third quarter performance as the company’s efforts to reinforce its market position and re-engage customers gains traction. The cosmetics retailer realized gains in both sales and profits and turned negative comparable sales in Q2 into a gain of 0.6% in Q3, better than expectations for another contraction of 2.0%.

The company earned a profit of $5.14 per share, up from $5.07 last year, and $0.62 better than expected. Revenue increased modestly, but was $30M higher than the street anticipated. Gross profit margin decreased slightly to 39.7% from 39.9% primarily on deleveraging of store and higher supply chain costs which were offset by favorable channel mix and lower inventory shrink.

For the remainder of the year which includes the rest of the holiday shopping season, Ulta (ULTA) raised its bottom-line sales outlook to a range of $11.1B to $11.2B from prior guidance of $11.0B to $11.2B and profit guidance to $23.20 to $23.75 per share from an earlier range of $22.60 to $23.50 per share. These forecasts compare favorably to the consensus estimates of $11.17B and $23.13, respectively.

The company also raised its operating margin from 12.7% - 13.0% to a new a range of 12.9% to 13.1%, and comparable sales to be down 1% to flat from earlier estimates of down 2% to flat. The consensus estimate called for and operating margin of 12.8% and comparable sales to be down 0.78%.

Ulta (ULTA) shares were last trading with a gain of 11.7%.

data.title: Unisys appoints Michael M. Thomson as chief executive officer
data.description:
• The Unisys (NYSE:UIS) elected Mike Thomson, current president and chief operating officer, to succeed Peter Altabef as the company's chief executive, effective April 1, 2025.
  • Thomson will join the company's board and will retain his current title as president.
  • Altabef, currently chair and CEO, will continue as chair of the board.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DocuSign in charts: Subscription revenue maintains single-digit growth pace in Q3
CyberArk announces secondary offering of ordinary shares
data.description:
• CyberArk (NASDAQ:CYBR) on Thursday said it has launched an underwritten secondary public offering by selling shareholder, of 1.14M shares of the company’s ordinary shares, par value NIS 0.01 per share.
  • The selling shareholder will receive all of the proceeds from the offering and the company will not receive any proceeds.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Victoria's Secret ups forecast, tops estimates for Q3 results on holiday season boost
data.description:
Victoria's Secret (NYSE:VSCO) on Thursday raised its full-year forecast and posted a results beat for the third quarter, driven by shopping momentum in the ongoing holiday season.

Shares of the lingerie retailer were down only marginally in aftermarket New York trading.

The company now expects 2024 net sales to be up about 1% to 2%, compared to prior guidance of down nearly 1%.

Adjusted operating income for the year is now forecast in the $315M to $345M range, compared to prior guidance of $275M to $300M, and far ahead of the Bloomberg consensus estimate of $295.5M.

In the third quarter, the company saw mid-single-digit growth in North America and over 20% growth from their international business.

"We are excited to see our momentum from the third quarter continue through Black Friday and Cyber Monday. Our merchandise offering and giftable product assortments are resonating with the customer and driving traffic both in stores and online," the company said in a statement.

Net loss for the quarter narrowed to $25.1M, compared to a net loss of $67.7M for the same period last year.

On an adjusted per-share basis, the company lost 40 cents, while analysts on average were expecting the company to lose 62 cents apiece.

Revenue rose 5.5% to $1.34B and beat consensus estimates.

data.title: Mistras Group appoints Natalia Shuman as president and CEO
data.description:
• Mistras Group (NYSE:MG) has announced that the Co.’s Board of Directors has appointed Natalia Shuman as MISTRAS Group’s new President and CEO, effective January 1, 2025.
  • Most recently, as Group Executive Vice President and Group Operating Council Member for Eurofins Scientific, she led over 12,000 employees, driving growth strategies, operational excellence, and strategic value creation.

data.title: Bit Digital bitcoin production slips 14% in November
data.description:
Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) produced 44.9 bitcoins (BTC-USD) in November, a 14% decline from its production in October, the company said Thursday.

Its active hash rate was ~2.51 EH/s, a 3.3% increase from last month.

The company had 266 servers (2,128 GPUs) actively generating revenue from Bit Digital AI contracts as of Nov. 30, 2024, earning $4.3M of total unaudited GPU Cloud revenue during the month. The amount earned was unchanged from October.

Treasury holdings of bitcoin (BTC-USD) were 813.0 with a fair market value of ~$78.4M, and of ethereum (ETH-USD) was 27,563.3 with a fair market value of $102.1M, as of Nov. 30, 2024. That's up from $54.8M FMV for bitcoin and $69.2M FMV for ether at the end of October.

The company had cash and cash equivalents of ~$332.5M, up from $203.9M at Oct. 31, 2024.

Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) stock increased 1.1% in Thursday after-hours trading, following a 9.2% drop in regular-hours trading.

data.title: Allegion declares $0.48 dividend
data.description:

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: DocuSign tops Q3 estimates, guides Q4 topline above consensus
Chevron sees ~$15B organic capex budget for 2025, $2B below this year
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) shares are little changed post-market Thursday after saying it forecasts a FY 2025 organic capital budget of $14.5B-$15.5B and expects to take $1.1B-$1.5B in charges during Q4 for restructuring, asset impairments and other obligations.

Chevron (CVX) said it sees Upstream spending at ~$13B, with about two-thirds allocated to develop its U.S. portfolio, although spending in the Permian Basin is expected to come in lower than the 2024 budget and anticipated at $4.5B-$5B as production growth is reduced in favor of free cash flow.

The company forecasts Downstream capex of ~$1.2B, with two-thirds allocated to the U.S.

Chevron (CVX) said it expects to spend less than $1B on the Tengiz oil field next year as the Kazakhstan expansion project nears completion.

Planned FY 2025 capex represents a $2B Y/Y reduction, and Chairman and CEO Mike Wirth said the "2025 capital budget along with our announced structural cost reductions demonstrate our commitment to cost and capital discipline."
